results.title: Making open hearth steel
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare, British Iron and Steel Federation and Merton Park
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “The Open hearth furnace series”.
Chemical reactions by which steel is refined from iron and steel scrap are explained by animated sequences. The function of slag and the differences between acid and basic furnaces are also outlined.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare, British Iron and Steel Federation and Merton Park

results.title: How it works
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare and Merton Park
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “The Open hearth furnace series”.
An explanation of how the furnace is constructed to withstand the very high temperatures needed to make steel. The system of regeneration by which gas and air are heated before being burned in the furnace is also illustrated.

group_works.title: The Writer in exile: James Joyce

results.title: The Writer in exile: James Joyce
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The 19th century novel”.
Graham Martin examines the theme of the writer in exile during the early part of the twentieth century, and takes as an example the Irish writer James Joyce. Denys Hawthorne, as James Joyce, reads from Joyce’s letters, poems and novels

group_siblings.title: Charles Dicken's expectations: a dream of childhood
group_siblings.creator_credit: Paul Kafno, BBC-TV and Open University

results.title: Charles Dicken's expectations: a dream of childhood
results.creator_credit: Paul Kafno, BBC-TV and Open University
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The 19th century novel”.
Graham Martin explores Pip’s character in terms of the surrender of childish dreams, and draws parallels with Dickens’ own life. The film is set on locations in Kent, at Dickens’ home at Gade Hill, and in London. Presented by Graham Martin, reader in literature. Voice of Charles Dickens read by Gabriel Woolf.

results.title: People in the country
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The New China No. 2”.
Shows views of life and work on a farming commune near Peking, varied rural scenes, temples and other places of interest, and National Day celebrations including a procession and folk dancing displays.

results.title: People in Peking
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The New China No. 1”.
Shows scenes in Peking, living conditions and daily life of industrial workers and their families, modern buildings, public parks and palaces maintained as showplaces, and National Day celebrations including callisthenic displays, human mosaic exhibitions and fireworks

group_works.title: Communicating with a group
group_works.creator_credit: John Krish and Rank Audio Visual

results.title: Communicating with a group
results.creator_credit: John Krish and Rank Audio Visual
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Manager in Action”.
This film takes the form of a dramatised in-company training session on group communication. The film is designed to improve the abilities of people to communicate effectively with all groups. The special problems of communication with groups are identified.
